senses.definition: following the fashion of the day and aware of the latest trends
a fashion or style that is popular at a particular time

examples.x: This trend has influenced how people dress at work.
This year's trend is oversized clothing.
The trend of minimalism has gained popularity recently.
Social media often spreads new fashion trends quickly.
Trends in fashion change rapidly every year.

senses.definition: fashion trends, music, decor, clothing, or styles from past decades, or inspired by them

examples.x: She loves shopping for retro at thrift stores, picking up classic pieces from the 70s and 80s.
Wearing retro has become a fashion statement, blending iconic styles from past decades with modern trends.
They specialize in selling retro that spans from mid-century furniture to 80s memorabilia.
His wardrobe is full of retro, featuring everything from old band tees to flared jeans.
Collecting retro has become a hobby for her, especially classic sunglasses and jackets from the 70s.
She decorated her living room with retro from the 60s, creating a cozy, vintage space.

senses.definition: suitable for friendly, relaxed, casual, or unofficial occasions and situations

examples.x: The club had an informal dress code, so jeans and t-shirts were acceptable.
The language in text messages is usually quite informal.
The music festival had an informal atmosphere where everyone could relax.
The party was an informal gathering of friends.
The staff had an informal celebration to mark the end of the project.

senses.definition: influenced by the latest or popular styles

examples.x: The trendy hairstyle she sported quickly became popular among her friends.
He likes to shop at trendy boutiques to keep his wardrobe fresh.
This season's trendy colors seem to be pastel shades and earthy tones.
Fashion magazines are full of articles about the latest trendy looks.
Trendy restaurants often feature innovative fusion cuisine.

senses.definition: a style of fashion marked by the deliberate choice of bland and casual clothes that do not draw attention
the quality of being fashionable and having style

examples.x: Her stylishness was evident in her effortlessly chic outfits that turned heads everywhere.
The café was known for its stylishness, with contemporary furniture and artistic décor.
He had a certain stylishness that made him stand out in any crowd, no matter the occasion.
The room exuded stylishness, with carefully chosen accents and a modern yet cozy vibe.
Her stylishness wasn't about trends; it was about confidence and timeless fashion.

senses.definition: a style of clothing that is tight-fitting and conforms closely to the body, often emphasizing the contours of the figure
neat, attractive and fashionable
not following the fashion of the day and unaware of the latest trends
making less attractive or favorable
(of a shirt) worn without a tie and not fastened at the neck

examples.x: The casual dress code allowed employees to wear open-necked shirts in the office.
She preferred the comfort of open-necked blouses during the warm summer months.
Attending the beach wedding, he chose an open-necked linen shirt to stay cool in the tropical weather.
The fashion-forward designer showcased a collection of open-necked dresses that exuded effortless elegance.

senses.definition: (of clothing) comfortable and suitable for everyday use or informal events and occasions

examples.x: She prefers casual attire for her weekends, often opting for jeans and t-shirts.
The office has a casual dress code on Fridays, allowing employees to wear more relaxed clothing.
His casual outfit consisted of a comfortable hoodie and sweatpants.
She wore a casual sundress to the picnic, enjoying the comfort and ease of movement.
He likes to keep it casual when meeting friends, usually wearing a simple polo shirt and shorts.

senses.definition: wearing clothes that are too casual or inadequate for a particular occasion
(of clothes, colors, hairstyles etc.) enhancing the wearer's appearance and making them more attractive
(of a person's style or clothes) dressy and fashionable, often conveying a sense of sophistication and elegance.

examples.x: The sharp dresser caught everyone's attention with his impeccable sense of style.
She appreciated a sharp outfit that made her feel confident and put-together for the job interview.

senses.definition: (of an object) making you feel relaxed because of it is warm or soft and does not hurt the body

examples.x: After a long day, I like to change into comfortable pajamas.
The sofa in the living room is so comfortable; I could relax on it for hours.
She prefers wearing comfortable sneakers when walking long distances.
The bed was made with soft, comfortable sheets that ensured a good night's sleep.
The car's comfortable seats made the long drive much more enjoyable.
He opted for a comfortable hoodie and sweatpants for the lazy Sunday afternoon.

senses.definition: (of clothes, furniture, etc.) unpleasant to use or wear

examples.x: The tight jeans were uncomfortable to wear for long periods.
The old chair was so uncomfortable that no one wanted to sit in it.
The scratchy fabric of the sweater made it uncomfortable against her skin.
The dining chairs were uncomfortable, causing guests to fidget during the meal.
She found the high heels uncomfortable to walk in, so she switched to flats.

senses.definition: (of clothes) stylish and suitable for formal occasions

examples.x: He chose a dressy suit for the dinner party to make a stylish impression.
She wore a dressy gown to the wedding, looking elegant and sophisticated.
The dressy attire for the event included cocktail dresses and suits.
She accessorized her dressy outfit with elegant jewelry and heels.
The dressy blouse paired perfectly with her tailored trousers for the business meeting.

senses.definition: having a refined and graceful appearance or style

examples.x: She wore an elegant gown to the gala, turning heads with her timeless beauty.
The bride looked stunning in her elegant wedding dress, radiating grace and charm as she walked down the aisle.
The bride's hairstyle was simple yet elegant, with cascading curls framing her face in soft waves.

senses.definition: attractive in a way that catches the eye

examples.x: She wore a fetching smile that brightened the room wherever she went.
He complimented her on her fetching outfit, admiring how it accentuated her figure.

senses.definition: (of clothes) made in a way that closely covers the body

examples.x: The fitted dress hugged her curves perfectly, accentuating her figure.
She slipped into the fitted jeans, feeling confident and stylish for her night out.
The fitted jacket completed the ensemble, adding a touch of elegance to her outfit.

senses.definition: exhibiting striking and showy characteristics, often characterized by extravagance or exuberance

examples.x: The mansion's flamboyant architecture featured elaborate embellishments and grandiose designs.
He was known for his flamboyant style of dress, often wearing vibrant colors and bold patterns.
The artist's flamboyant paintings were characterized by bold strokes of color and intricate patterns.
She made a flamboyant entrance at the party, turning heads with her extravagant gown and sparkling accessories.
The hotel lobby was adorned with flamboyant artwork and luxurious furnishings, creating an atmosphere of opulence and grandeur that impressed even the most discerning guests.

senses.definition: suitable for fancy, important, serious, or official occasions and situations

examples.x: Formal education typically takes place in schools or universities.
It's important to use formal language in a job interview.
The conference had a formal atmosphere with everyone dressed in suits.
The school requires a formal uniform during weekdays.
The students had to follow a formal process to apply for a scholarship.

senses.definition: (of clothing) fitting the body tightly in a way that the contours of the figure are clearly seen
unfashionable, outdated, and unattractive, often giving a sloppy appearance

examples.x: She felt frumpy in her oversized sweater and baggy jeans.
Despite her best efforts, her outfit looked frumpy and out of date.
The frumpy old couch clashed with the modern decor of the room.
She avoided wearing the frumpy dress that made her feel self-conscious.
The frumpy hat she wore did little to shield her from the sun.

senses.definition: neat and stylish
plain and not brightly colored

examples.x: She wore a sober dress to the formal dinner.
The living room was decorated in sober tones of gray and beige.
His sober outfit reflected his serious personality.
The artist preferred sober colors for her minimalist paintings.
The company's logo is designed in sober shades of blue and white.

senses.definition: (of clothes) stylish, attractive, and suitable for sports

examples.x: She opted for a sporty look with a fitted tracksuit and sleek running shoes.
His sporty jacket had a modern design and was perfect for outdoor activities.
The brand is known for its sporty apparel, which combines comfort with style.
She loves wearing sporty leggings and tank tops for her workout sessions.
He chose a sporty cap and sunglasses to complete his athleisure outfit.

senses.definition: appealing in a way that is fashionable and elegant

examples.x: The boutique specializes in offering stylish clothing and accessories for fashion-forward individuals.
She always dresses in a stylish manner, effortlessly combining trends with her own unique flair.
His apartment is decorated in a stylish, minimalist aesthetic, with clean lines and modern furniture.
The new restaurant in town has a stylish interior design, with chic decor and comfortable seating.

senses.definition: in a manner that reflects a sense of fashion, elegance, or sophistication

examples.x: She dressed stylishly, combining modern trends with a touch of classic elegance.
The room was decorated stylishly, with a blend of contemporary and timeless elements.
The magazine featured articles on how to live stylishly in a small space.
The furniture in the boutique was designed stylishly, appealing to a modern aesthetic.

senses.definition: having an appealing appearance that is stylish

examples.x: She always looks chic in her trendy outfits and minimalist accessories.
The chic interior design of the apartment impressed all the guests.
Despite being on a budget, she managed to create a chic and fashionable wardrobe.
His chic hairstyle and well-groomed appearance made him stand out in the crowd.
The chic boutique offered a curated selection of high-end fashion brands.
She looked effortlessly chic in her black dress and matching heels.

senses.definition: (used especially of clothes) showy in a way that is tasteless
stylish, attractive, and often associated with luxury or sophistication

examples.x: She looked absolutely glamorous in her evening gown and sparkling jewelry.
The glamorous party was filled with celebrities and VIP guests.
Her glamorous lifestyle included jet-setting to exotic destinations and attending exclusive events.
The glamorous decor of the hotel lobby exuded an air of opulence and grandeur.
His glamorous sports car turned heads as he drove through the city streets.

senses.definition: related to the fashion style associated with grunge rock music, consisting loose torn jeans and layers of clothing
(of a piece of clothing) casual and loose, without much shape
(of people or clothes) looking neat, tidy, and elegantly fashionable

examples.x: He looked particularly smart in his tailored suit and polished shoes for the business meeting.
The bride’s dress was not only beautiful but also had a smart design that highlighted her figure perfectly.
She prefers wearing smart attire to the office, believing it boosts her confidence and professionalism.
For the gala, everyone was expected to dress smart, so many opted for evening gowns and tuxedos.
The smart outfit she chose for the interview made a great first impression on her potential employer.

senses.definition: (of clothes) neat, conventional and rather informal
in a neat and stylish way
wearing clothes that are too formal or excessive for a particular occasion
too bright and colorful in a way that is tasteless

examples.x: The garish decorations at the party clashed with the elegant atmosphere of the venue.
She regretted wearing the garish outfit, which attracted more criticism than compliments.
The garish neon signs lit up the street, making it feel overwhelming at night.
His room was filled with garish posters and mismatched furniture, lacking any cohesive style.
The artist's use of garish colors in the painting was intended to provoke a strong reaction.

senses.definition: informal clothing that the urban youth of a particular subculture typically wear
improving or emphasizing someone's good features, making them appear more attractive
too bright in a distasteful way

examples.x: His suit was loud, with neon colors and bold patterns that caught everyone’s eye.
The room was decorated in a loud style, with bright wallpaper and mismatched furniture.
His loud sneakers made him stand out wherever he went.
The festival was filled with people in loud outfits, covered in sequins and neon colors.
She preferred loud shoes, often with glitter or flashy details, to complete her look.

senses.definition: (of clothes) loose and not fitting the body tightly

examples.x: She preferred wearing baggy jeans for comfort during long flights.
His baggy sweater kept him warm during the chilly evening.
The teenager's baggy shirt gave off a casual, laid-back vibe.

senses.definition: lacking a particular style, fashion, or design
